title: DQ Platform _owners.yaml v3
description: Canonical JSON Schema for rules/_owners.yaml at schema version 3. v3 extends v2 with an optional per-entity `onboarding` boolean field (per ADR-0046). When `entity.onboarding == true` AND `env.OnboardingChannel` is non-empty, the alerting consumer routes the alert to OnboardingChannel instead of `entity.channels` — this closes ADR-0040's shared-substrate channel-collision procedural workaround. v3 is rules-side only; no engine-side mirror exists per ADR-0022's carrying convention (owners metadata is consumed by the alerting layer, not the engine query path).

schema_version.description: Owners schema version. Const 3 at this contract level; schema evolution follows ADR-0006 CC12 (additive within a major; breaking change requires a v4 file).

entities.description: Map of entity identifier to owner descriptor. Keys must match the entity-identifier pattern committed in rules/_schema/v2.schema.json (the rule schema's entity pattern). The linter cross-checks that every rule YAML's entity field is a key here per ADR-0006 CC9, and that the rule's mode equals the entry's mode per ADR-0021.

owner.description: Human-readable owner identifier (team name, group alias, or rotation). Free-form string; the engine does not interpret it.

mode.description: Evaluation mode the entity runs under (per ADR-0021 §C-B0S1.3). The linter cross-checks that every rule YAML for this entity carries the same mode value.

onboarding.description: When true AND `env.OnboardingChannel` is non-empty, the alerting consumer routes alerts for this entity to OnboardingChannel instead of `channels` (per ADR-0046). Default false (absent). Operators flip this boolean while an entity is in Tier 1 (Test-soak) per ADR-0040; flipping it back to false at Tier 2 promotion. Replaces the procedural qa-prefix-rename workaround from ADR-0040 §Tier 0 → Tier 1 criterion 6 / §Tier 1 → Tier 2 criterion 7.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the entity (purpose, upstream provider, etc.). The engine does not interpret it.

channels.description: Routing map keyed by alert category (data_quality / operational). Each value is a non-empty array of channel references in the form `<type>:<id>`, where <type> is a short lowercase token (e.g., slack, email, pagerduty) and <id> is the channel-specific identifier. The engine does not validate <type> beyond the lexical form here; the alerting consumer (per ADR-0006 CC3) decides which channel types it can route to. NOTE per ADR-0046: when `entity.onboarding == true` and `env.OnboardingChannel` is non-empty, this list is overridden by the env-level onboarding channel; otherwise normal ADR-0006 CC7 routing applies.

items.description: Channel reference in the form `<type>:<id>`. The colon separator is the wire encoding committed by Phase 5 per ADR-0006 CC2's deferral to Wave 3. <type> is a short lowercase token (slack, email, pagerduty, …); <id> is the channel-specific identifier (Slack channel name like #dq-foo, email address like oncall@example.com, PagerDuty service ID, …). The id may contain any non-whitespace character, including #, @, /, since channel naming is substrate-specific.

severityOverrides.description: Optional map of category -> environment -> severity. The outer key is the alert category (data_quality / operational). The inner key is the environment name (free-form lowercase token; the engine does not enumerate environments at this schema level). The value is one of the ADR-0006 CC6 severity enum values. Absent entries default to the engine deployment's per-category severity default per ADR-0006 CC3.
